content_en: How can your eyes have their fill of sleep when there are events which are awakening every sleeper? When your brothers in Syria cannot even nap Except on the backs of horses or in the bellies of vultures? The Byzantines are humiliating them whilst you Live a life of luxury like a man averse to combat. Here the poet is addressing those who have stayed away from fighting alongside their Muslim brothers in Syria. He begins this portion of his poem with a heated call to the Muslims, 'O sons of Islam, wake up from your sleep, for this invasion is coming to you and it will bring low your elite.' Then he wonders about them and their sleep: how can they sleep peacefully, enjoy a life of luxury and feel safe, when not far away terrible things are befalling their 'brothers in Syria' and they cannot find even a few moments in which to take a nap in their houses; most of the time they are on horseback, fighting, or the decree of martyrdom has overtaken them and they are snatched by the vultures, as they have no one to bury their bodies, or they may fall into the hands of their Frankish enemies and be humiliated. 'But it seems that you are enjoying a life of ease and are either surrendering or allying yourself with your enemy.' How much blood was shed and how many young Muslim girls were killed whilst trying to cover their beauty with their hands out of shyness. Silver swords turned red and spearheads dripped with blood In the midst of stabbing and striking which made the heads of young boys turn grey. These are such battles that those who keep away to remain safe and sound will regret it bitterly. The hands of the polytheists have unsheathed the swords, but they will be sheathed again-in their own chests and skulls. And you can almost hear him who is buried in Taybah (Madinah)y calling out in the loudest voice, O Family of Hishdm! In these lines, the poet depicts the ferocity of the battles which took place between the Muslims and their Frankish enemies, in which the blood of many Muslims was spilled and women's seclusion was transgressed upon, but they could not find anything with which to protect their chaste bodies except their arms, which they held up out of shyness and fear. These battles grew intense, with a great deal of killing, until the edges of the swords and spears appeared to glow red hot, and children's hair would turn grey because of the horrific scenes of stabbing and striking that they saw. Then he again alerts those who stay behind, and warns them that they will regret not participating in these battles, warning again of their dangers and mocking the enemy by saying that the sharp swords that they have unsheathed will come back to them, buried in their own chests and skulls. In the final lines, he reaffirms how terrifying these wars are by saying that the (Messenger (SAAW), from his tomb in Madinah, is summoning the Arabs and Muslims, not only the clan of Hisham, to help in the fight against the enemy.157 Seljuk leaders of Jihad prior to 'Imad ad-Deen Zangi It is a well known fact in the history of the Crusades that the Islamic Jihad against the Crusaders in the Muslim East first emerged in Mesopotamia, which is the region between the Tigris and the Euphrates, close to Syria, including Diyar Mudar and Diyarbakr (in Turkish, Diyarbakr). It is called al-Jazeerah 158 in Arabic because it is located between the two rivers of the Tigris and the Euphrates. The Mesopotamian region is noted for its fresh air, suitability for agriculture and abundant crops. In it there are many great cities, strong fortresses and citadels.159 Among the reasons why the resistance movement first emerged in the Mesopotamian region are the following: a) Mesopotamia was the first region in the Muslim East to be touched by the fire of the Crusader menace, when the Crusaders seized Edessa and established the first of the Crusader kingdoms there in 490 AH/1097 CE. The inhabitants realized the danger posed by the Crusader penetration into their country, which led the Muslims to think seriously about attacking the Crusaders. b) The character of the Mesopotamian region had been shaped in the early days of Islam, because it was located on the edges of the Byzantine state, which posed a great danger to the Muslims during the days of the Umayyad and Abbasid periods. Hence it became the first line of defence on the Muslim borders against the Byzantines. After the Crusader invasion, Mesopotamia faced the Crusader Kingdom of Edessa, which posed the greatest danger to the Abbasid caliphate in Baghdad. c) In the second half of the fifth century AH (eleventh century CE), Mesopotamia witnessed the appearance of the Seljuk Turks who were famous for their love of raising horses and their adventurous spirit, as well as their great zeal for Islam; they were new converts to the faith, and adhered to the Sunni school of thought.
content_id:
Bagaimana matamu bisa terpuaskan dengan tidur, padahal ada peristiwa-peristiwa yang membangunkan setiap orang yang tertidur? Ketika saudara-saudaramu di Syam tidak bisa sekadar beristirahat kecuali di atas punggung kuda atau di perut burung nasar? Byzantium menghinakan mereka sementara engkau hidup dalam kemewahan seperti orang yang enggan berperang. Di sini sang penyair menyapa mereka yang menjauh dari pertempuran bersama saudara-saudara Muslim mereka di Syam. Ia memulai bagian syairnya ini dengan seruan yang berapi-api kepada umat Islam: 'Wahai putra-putra Islam, bangkitlah dari tidurmu, karena invasi ini mendekat dan akan merendahkan kaum elitemu.' Kemudian ia bertanya-tanya tentang mereka dan tidur mereka: bagaimana mereka bisa tidur dengan tenang, menikmati kemewahan, dan merasa aman, sementara tidak jauh dari sana hal-hal yang mengerikan menimpa 'saudara-saudara mereka di Syam' yang bahkan tidak bisa menemukan beberapa saat untuk beristirahat di rumah mereka; sebagian besar waktu mereka berada di atas kuda, bertempur, atau ketentuan syahid telah menyusul mereka dan mereka disambar burung nasar karena tidak ada yang menguburkan jenazah mereka, atau mereka mungkin jatuh ke tangan musuh Frank dan dihinakan. Namun tampaknya engkau menikmati hidup yang nyaman dan entah menyerah atau bersekutu dengan musuhmu.

Betapa banyak darah tertumpah dan betapa banyak gadis-gadis Muslim muda yang terbunuh ketika berusaha menutupi kecantikan mereka dengan tangan mereka karena rasa malu. Pedang-pedang perak memerah dan ujung-ujung tombak menetes darah di tengah tikaman dan pukulan yang membuat rambut bocah-bocah muda memutih. Inilah pertempuran-pertempuran yang membuat mereka yang menjauh demi keselamatan akan menyesal dengan pahit. Tangan-tangan kaum musyrik telah menghunus pedang, namun pedang itu akan kembali tersarung — di dada dan tengkorak mereka sendiri. Dan hampir saja kamu mendengar orang yang dikuburkan di Taybah (Madinah) berseru dengan suara paling keras, Wahai Keluarga Hisyam! Dalam baris-baris ini, sang penyair menggambarkan keganasan pertempuran yang terjadi antara umat Islam dan musuh-musuh Frank mereka, di mana darah banyak Muslim tertumpah dan kehormatan para wanita dilanggar, namun mereka tidak menemukan apa pun untuk melindungi tubuh mereka yang terhormat kecuali lengan mereka yang mereka angkat karena rasa malu dan ketakutan. Pertempuran-pertempuran ini semakin sengit dengan banyak pembunuhan hingga bilah pedang dan tombak tampak memerah membara, dan rambut anak-anak menjadi beruban karena pemandangan tikaman dan pukulan yang mengerikan yang mereka saksikan.

Para pemimpin Seljuk dalam Jihad sebelum Imad ad-Din Zangi merupakan fakta yang dikenal dalam sejarah Perang Salib bahwa jihad Islam melawan Tentara Salib di Timur Muslim pertama kali muncul di Mesopotamia, yakni wilayah antara Sungai Tigris dan Efrat, dekat dengan Syam, termasuk Diyar Mudar dan Diyarbakir. Wilayah Mesopotamia dikenal dengan udara segar, kesesuaian untuk pertanian, dan hasil panen yang melimpah. Di sana terdapat banyak kota-kota besar, benteng-benteng yang kokoh, dan sitadel. Di antara alasan mengapa gerakan perlawanan pertama kali muncul di wilayah Mesopotamia adalah: pertama, Mesopotamia adalah wilayah pertama di Timur Muslim yang tersentuh api ancaman Tentara Salib, ketika Tentara Salib merebut Edessa dan mendirikan kerajaan Salib pertama di sana pada tahun 490 H/1097 M. Para penghuninya menyadari bahaya yang ditimbulkan oleh penetrasi Tentara Salib ke negeri mereka, yang mendorong umat Islam untuk serius memikirkan serangan terhadap Tentara Salib. Kedua, karakter wilayah Mesopotamia telah terbentuk sejak awal Islam karena terletak di tepi negara Byzantium, yang menimbulkan bahaya besar bagi umat Islam pada masa Dinasti Umayyah dan Abbasiyah. Karenanya ia menjadi garis pertahanan pertama di perbatasan Muslim melawan Byzantium. Setelah invasi Tentara Salib, Mesopotamia menghadapi Kerajaan Salib Edessa yang menimbulkan ancaman terbesar bagi Khilafah Abbasiyah di Baghdad. Ketiga, pada paruh kedua abad kelima Hijriah, Mesopotamia menyaksikan kemunculan bangsa Turki Seljuk yang terkenal dengan kecintaan mereka pada kuda dan semangat petualangan, serta antusiasme mereka yang besar terhadap Islam; mereka adalah muallaf baru yang berpegang pada mazhab Sunni.
